Question: Project execution has just begun. As the project manager, you want to ensure the work is being performed according to the required quality standards. What is your best course of action?
Answer A: Implement the quality management plan using the appropriate tools and techniques
Answer B: Verify that the project deliverables conform to the standards established in the quality
Answer C: Develop the quality management plan and define the quality standards for the project and
Answer D: Obtain feedback from relevant stakeholders to evaluate their satisfaction

Explanation: The question states your project is in execution, and the scenario implies you want to carry out the Manage Quality process. During the Manage Quality process, quality activities are performed according to the quality management plan which was developed to incorporate the required quality standards. Therefore, to ensure the work is being performed according to the required quality standards, you need to implement the quality management plan using the tools and techniques specified in the plan. T
Details for Answer A: Implement the quality management plan using the appropriate tools and techniquesCorrect. During Manage Quality, which according to the scenario is the process you are going to carry out, quality activities are performed according to the quality management plan which was developed to incorporate the required quality standards. To ensure the work is being performed according to the required quality
Details for Answer B: Verify that the project deliverables conform to the standards established in the qualitymanagement plan Incorrect. Verifying the project deliverables is performed in the Control Quality process to determine the correctness of the deliverables. According to the scenario, you want to ensure the work is being performed according to the required quality standards rather than determine the correctness
Details for Answer C: Develop the quality management plan and define the quality standards for the project andits products Incorrect. Developing the quality management plan is the main activity of the Plan Quality Management process which is part of the Planning Process Group. The scenario suggests the project is execution; therefore, the quality management plan has already been developed.
Details for Answer D: Obtain feedback from relevant stakeholders to evaluate their satisfactionIncorrect. Obtaining feedback from stakeholders to evaluate their satisfaction is typically performed as part of the Close Project or Phase process after the customer has accepted the completed deliverable. In the scenario, the project is in execution and concerned with the quality of the work being performed. Therefore, obta

Question: project is divided into four sequential phases. The second phase of the project has just been completed, and the third phase is about to begin. What should the project manager do first?
Answer A: new phase should begin with the Initiating Process Group.
Answer B: Perform the process of identifying the project stakeholders
Answer C: Start the process of monitoring and controlling the project work
Answer D: Begin the process of developing the project management plan
Hint: **A new phase should begin with the Initiating Process Group.** Correct Answer: B
Explanation: The process of identifying project stakeholders occurs for the first time in a project either before or at the same time the project charter is developed and approved. It is repeated as necessary but should be performed at the start of each phase and when a significant change in the project or organization occurs. Each time the identification process is repeated, the project management plan components and project documents should be consulted to identify relevant project stakeholders.
Details for Answer A: Begin the process of directing and managing the project workIncorrect. A project or a new phase of an existing project is started with the Initiating Process Group. The Direct and Manage Project Work process is part of the Executing --- Process Group. Therefore, this answer choice can be eliminated.
Details for Answer B: Perform the process of identifying the project stakeholdersCorrect. The Initiating Process Group is made up of two project management processes, Develop Project Charter and Identify Stakeholders. The Initiating Process Group is performed to define a new project or a new phase of an existing project by obtaining authorization to start the project or phase. Of the available options, only the Identif
Details for Answer C: Start the process of monitoring and controlling the project workIncorrect. The Monitor and Control Project Work process is part of the Monitoring and Controlling Process Group. The phase must be initiated, planned, and execution must be underway before there can be any project work to monitor and control.
Details for Answer D: Begin the process of developing the project management planIncorrect. The Develop Project Management Plan process is part of the Planning Process Group. A phase is started with the Initiating Process Group and not the Planning Process Group, making this an incorrect answer.

Answer D: Conduct future software development projects with only internal resources

Explanation: The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used to improve the performance of the current project. At the end of the project, the information contained in the lessons learned register is then transferred to an organizational process asset called the lessons learned repository for use in future projects. Any information regarding claims and how they were resolved should be documented in the lessons learned register to avoi
Details for Answer A: Record the final resolution of the problem in the issue logIncorrect. It is not known from the question whether the problem was captured in the issue log. Assuming it was, then the final resolution should be recorded as a project document update. However, the issue log will not make the information available for use in future projects, so this action is not the best way to prevent a recurrence of
Details for Answer B: Capture the situation and its resolution in the lessons learned registerCorrect. Information regarding any claims and how they were resolved should be documented in the lessons learned register to avoid recurrences in the future.
Details for Answer C: Update the risk register with the implementation of the risk responseIncorrect. The question does not specify if the risk of a computer virus was identified earlier in the project. If this risk was captured in the risk register, then it may be updated with status changes and finally closed out. However, the risk register from the current project does not serve as an input for future projects, and
Details for Answer D: Conduct future software development projects with only internal resourcesIncorrect. Using only internal resources would allow more control over the project. Question: project manager is in the process of developing the risk management plan. The project manager has just pulled a blank template of a risk breakdown structure (RBS) from the organizational process assets. What should the project manager do next?
Answer B: Develop a comprehensive list of project risks
Answer D: Develop responses to individual project risks
Hint: Which answer choice describes an activity associated with an element of the risk management plan? Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The question implies that the Plan Risk Management process is underway with the development of the risk management plan. One of the elements of the risk management plan is risk categories. Risk categories provide a means for grouping individual project risks. A common way to structure risk categories is with a risk breakdown structure (RBS), which is a hierarchical representation of potential sources of risk. An RBS helps the project team consider the full range of sources from which individual
Details for Answer A: Determine the risk categoriesCorrect. One of the elements of the risk management plan is risk categories. Risk categories provide a means for grouping individual project risks. A common way to structure risk categories is with a risk breakdown structure (RBS).
Details for Answer B: Develop a comprehensive list of project risksIncorrect. The development of the risk management plan indicates that the Plan Risk Management process is underway. A comprehensive list of project risks is developed during the Identify Risks process which follows the Plan Risk Management process.
Details for Answer C: Create the risk registerIncorrect. The question implies that the Plan Risk Management process is being performed. The risk register is created as an output of the Identify Risks process, rather than the Plan Risk Management process.
Details for Answer D: Develop responses to individual project risksIncorrect. The development of responses to individual project risks is part of the Plan Risk Responses process which comes later in project risk planning. The scenario describes the development of the risk management plan which precedes risk identification and the development of the responses to those risks.

Question: project manager has encountered a new issue during project execution and wants to document this issue in the issue log. What should the project manager do first?
Answer A: Add the issue to the issue log
Answer B: Submit a change request to update the issue log
Answer C: Analyze the impact of the issue and submit a change request to update the issue log
Answer D: Consult the issue management plan on how to manage new issues on the project
Hint: The issue log is not a part of the project management plan. Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The issue log is a project document used by the project teams to document issues they encounter on the project. Unlike the project management plan, which is a controlled document, the issue log is a project document and not a part of the project management plan. To be updated, the issue log does not require a change request. When a new issue arises on the project, the project manager should first add the issue to the issue log, assign an owner and a target resolution date. The issue log should t
Details for Answer A: Add the issue to the issue logCorrect. When a new issue arises throughout the course of a project, the issue should be added to the issue log.
Details for Answer B: Submit a change request to update the issue logIncorrect. The issue log is not a controlled document and does not require a change request to be updated.
Details for Answer C: Analyze the impact of the issue and submit a change request to update the issue logIncorrect. While an impact of the issue on the project can and should be analyzed, and any further actions taken, entering the issue into the issue log should be the first step. Again, the issue log is not a controlled document and does not require a change request to be updated.
Details for Answer D: Consult the issue management plan on how to manage new issues on the projectIncorrect. 'Issue management plan' is a fake term made up for this question.

Question: project manager has been authorized to apply organizational resources to a new project. What should the project manager do next?
Hint: Try to eliminate the activities, which have already been done. Correct Answer: B

By saying that the project manager has been authorized to apply organizational resources to a new project, the question suggests that the project charter has been created and approved. The summary milestone schedule is part of the project charter and, therefore, should have already been developed.
Details for Answer B: Create the assumption logCorrect. It can be implied from the scenario, that the project is in the Develop Project Charter process. In addition to the project charter, the assumption log is an output of this process.
Details for Answer C: Develop the project business documentsIncorrect. The project business documents include the business case and benefits management plan. These documents are created during pre-project work and serve as inputs into the Develop Project Charter process inferred by the scenario. Since the question implies that the project charter has already been approved, the business documents would have been develop
Details for Answer D: Finalize the project charterIncorrect. The project charter provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities. Question: You are in the planning stage of a large project, which will require contracts with various vendors and suppliers to produce the project's deliverables. With the procurement management plan and make-or-buy analysis complete, you are about to begin the development of a document that will outline the procurement strategy for the project. Which of the following are you least likely to include in this document?
Hint: The question is asking for the element that is 'least' likely to be included in the procurement strategy. One of the answer choices should be included in the procurement management plan rather than the procurement strategy. Correct Answer: C
Explanation: The procurement strategy is determined as part of the Plan Procurement Management process. The procurement strategy, an output from this process, is comprised of three elements which include procurement delivery methods, type of agreements, and procurement phases. The question is asking for the element which is 'least' likely to be included in the procurement strategy. The responsibilities of stakeholders, as it pertains to procurements, is an element of the procurement management plan rather th
Details for Answer A: Procurement delivery methodsIncorrect. This answer choice represents one of the three elements typically included in the procurement strategy.
Details for Answer B: Type of agreementsIncorrect. The type of agreements is one of the three components that are typically included in the procurement strategy.
Details for Answer C: Responsibilities of stakeholdersCorrect. The responsibilities of stakeholders, as it pertains to procurements, is an element of the procurement management plan rather than the procurement strategy.
Details for Answer D: Procurement phasesIncorrect. Procurement phases are generally included in the procurement strategy.

Question: You and your team are meeting to evaluate vendor proposals. One of the team members previously worked with Company A and praised their professionalism and quality. Another team member prefers Company B. Several other team members join the conversation by adding their opinions. What should you remind the team members to focus on to select a seller?
Hint: Which procurement document is used in the evaluation of vendor proposals? Correct Answer: C
Explanation: Source selection criteria are a set of measurable attributes desired by the buyer which a seller is required to meet or exceed to be selected for a contract. By focusing on source selection criteria, a proposal can be evaluated quantitatively, thereby removing any personal biases.
Details for Answer A: Procurement statement of work (SOW)Incorrect. The procurement SOW provides sellers with a clearly stated set of goals, requirements, and outcomes from which they can produce a proposal for the work they will provide. In this scenario, the sellers' proposals have already been received. Therefore, this --- is not a correct answer.
Details for Answer B: Bid documentsIncorrect. Bid documents are used to solicit proposals from prospective sellers. In this scenario, the vendors' proposals have already been received. Therefore, this is not the correct answer.
Details for Answer C: Source selection criteriaCorrect. Source selection criteria are measurable and provide a quantitative basis for evaluation of proposals thereby minimizing personal biases. The source selection criteria are the best thing for your project team to focus on when choosing the vendor.
Details for Answer D: Procurement management planIncorrect. The procurement management plan details the activities to be undertaken during the procurement process. Source selection criteria are part of the procurement documents, not part of the procurement management plan. Therefore, this is not a correct answer.

Question: project manager provides overall direction, training, and support to her cross-functional team, but entrusts the team to manage and track their own work assignments and make decisions as a group. The project manager participates in regular team meetings to monitor the team's progress and provide support, but generally stays out of discussions about workload or team decision making. What is the project manager employing to manage the team?
Hint: This methodology is commonly associated with agile project management frameworks. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: This is an example of a self-organizing team, where the project manager provides overall direction and support while entrusting team members to make their own decisions and manage the work. The other answer choices may be part of the project manager's management approach, but self-organizing teams is the better answer choice.
Details for Answer A: Emotional intelligenceIncorrect. Although the project manager may be using emotional intelligence to a certain --- degree, that is not the primary technique or methodology she is applying to manage the team.
Details for Answer B: Self-organizing teamsCorrect. Self-organizing teams are often used for projects using agile approaches. In this scenario, the project manager and the team have roles that are typical of a self-organizing team. The project manager provides overall direction and support while entrusting the team to manage their own work to get the job done.
Details for Answer C: Team buildingIncorrect. Team building is conducting activities that enhance the team’s social relations and build a collaborative and cooperative working environment. Although the project manager is cultivating an environment that supports team building, this scenario is best described as an example of a self-organizing team.
Details for Answer D: MotivationIncorrect. Motivation is providing a reason for someone to act. Teams are motivated by empowering them to participate in decision making and encouraging them to work independently. Although this is an attractive answer choice because the project manager is motivating the team to work independently and make their own decisions, the method he is using to empower and motivate them is a self

Question: You are managing a new needs assessment project. While you are analyzing the monthly progress data your team has submitted, you notice that one of your project team members has spent half as much time working on a project activity than you had planned. What should you do?
Answer A: Reward the team member for finishing the activity ahead of schedule
Answer B: Investigate further to determine if there is a problem
Answer C: Issue a corrective action as you obviously overestimated the activity duration
Answer D: Do nothing now and wait for the next month's progress data.
Hint: The Monitor and Control Project Work process balances the demands of the all the knowledge areas to control the project. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: The Monitor and Control Project Work process is part of Integration Management. The project manager must balance the demands of all the knowledge areas to control the project. You can't get a good understanding of how the project is performing against the project plan by only monitoring one aspect of the project, for example, one employee's activity report. Therefore, further investigation needs to be completed before acting on data from one process.
Details for Answer A: Reward the team member for finishing the activity ahead of scheduleIncorrect. You certainly want to reward employees for good work, but at this point, you --- don't know if the activity was completed correctly or if the team member completed other activities on time. A further investigation would be warranted.
Details for Answer B: Investigate further to determine if there is a problemCorrect. You can't get a good understanding of how the project is performing against the project plan by only monitoring one aspect of the project. You should review both the quality of the deliverables produced by the team member during the activity and whether she met other planned milestones. Once you have investigated further, you will know
Details for Answer C: Issue a corrective action as you obviously overestimated the activity durationIncorrect. Just because the team member worked half the hours, you had planned for her to work on an activity doesn't mean you estimated incorrectly. Perhaps there are other reasons for the discrepancy. Once you have investigated further, you will know if there is a discrepancy between your planned and actual estimates.
Details for Answer D: Do nothing now and wait for the next month's progress data.Incorrect. A project manager should be proactive. Waiting a month for new progress data could allow a problem to worsen, if there is a problem, making this an incorrect choice.

Question: You are managing the development of a new user interface. You have produced the first interim deliverable for the project, which is currently being evaluated by the quality control department. Last weekend, at the company barbecue, you spoke with several business development executives and asked them what they thought about the interim deliverable. They shared how they were satisfied with the deliverable and were pleased with the project's progress. What best describes what you have done?
Hint: Part of monitoring stakeholder engagement is consistently knowing their position on the project. Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Maintaining stakeholder relationships and understanding stakeholders' perceptions of the project's progress is accomplished in the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process. Monitor Stakeholder Engagement is the process of monitoring project stakeholder relationships and tailoring strategies for engaging stakeholders through modification of engagement strategies and plans. Meetings, either formal or informal, can be used as a tool in the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process. In this scenario, you
Details for Answer A: Scope validationIncorrect. Scope validation is the process of formally accepting a project deliverable. Before a deliverable can be accepted, it must have gone through the Control Quality process. In this scenario, the deliverable is in the process of being verified; therefore, it is not available for scope validation. Aside from that, scope validation is a formal process; it wouldn't be performed
Details for Answer B: Verification of the interim deliverableIncorrect. The Control Quality process determines the correctness of deliverables resulting in verified deliverables. In the scenario described, the deliverable is in the process of being evaluated; therefore, it has not yet been verified.
Details for Answer C: Managing qualityIncorrect. Managing quality focuses on implementing the quality management plan into executable quality activities, identifying ineffective processes and causes of poor quality neither of which are mentioned in the scenario.
Details for Answer D: Monitoring stakeholder engagementCorrect. Monitoring stakeholder engagement helps determine the position of stakeholder groups and individuals at any particular time in the project. The business development executives, in this scenario, are sharing their satisfaction with the project progress and interest in the interim deliverable, thereby sharing their levels of engagement.

Question: project manager is leading a product development project, which will use an agile framework. The project team and other stakeholders are co-located at the company's corporate office. What is the best way for the project manager to communicate the project status and promote transparency?

Answer D: Display burndown charts and other relevant project artifacts in a highly visible location
Hint: How might daily project status updates be communicated using a form of push communications? Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Agile best practices promote transparency. Displaying burndown charts and other relevant project information in a highly visible location promotes transparency as current information regarding project status is readily available to everyone. All of the answer choices represent actions that should be taken by the project manager. The PMP aspirant should select the 'best' answer among the available options. With the project team and stakeholders co-located at the corporate office, posting the proj
Details for Answer A: Reserve a conference room and invite all relevant stakeholders to monthly project statusmeetings Incorrect. Holding monthly project status meetings is a good idea. However, the project status will change daily during project execution. A monthly meeting will only provide a snapshot of the project status. To promote transparency as asked by the question, it is better to make current information rea
Details for Answer B: Post burndown charts and other project status documents to the company's intranet siteIncorrect. The project manager probably should post any relevant project status documents to the company's intranet site. However, this action requires the project team and stakeholders to proactively access the information rather than having the information readily available to them.
Details for Answer C: Share the appropriate information whenever requested by stakeholdersIncorrect. The project manager should share any relevant information upon request. However, requiring stakeholders to seek out the information they need is not the best way to keep stakeholders informed and promote transparency.
Details for Answer D: Display burndown charts and other relevant project artifacts in a highly visible locationCorrect. Agile best practices encourage transparency. Displaying burndown charts and other relevant project information in a highly visible location promotes transparency as the information readily available to everyone.

Quality audits, often performed during the Manage Quality process, identify project processes that have gaps or shortcomings and are,
Details for Answer A: Cost of nonconformance, prevention costs--- Incorrect. Question: company is considering two projects, project A with the internal rate of return (IRR) of 25% and project B with the IRR of 15%. Which project should the company select and why?
Answer A: Project B because a lower IRR represents lower costs
Answer B: Project A because it has a higher IRR
Answer C: Project B because a lower IRR represents a shorter payback period
Answer D: Project A because letter "A" comes before "B"
Hint: The internal rate of return (IRR) is the interest rate at which the cash inflow and cash outflow of the project equal zero. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: The internal rate of return (IRR) is the interest rate at which the cash inflow and cash outflow of the project equal zero. The IRR can be an important metric for benefits analysis. The PMP exam will not likely ask you to calculate the IRR. However, two important concepts should be understood for the exam. First, a positive IRR means a positive rate of return. Second, all other things being equal or no other details are provided, a project with a higher IRR is typically selected over a project w
Details for Answer A: Project B because a lower IRR represents lower costsIncorrect. IRR is an internal rate of return. There is no correlation between the IRR and costs.
Details for Answer B: Project A because it has a higher IRRCorrect. The internal rate of return (IRR) is the interest rate at which the cash inflow and cash outflow of the project equal zero. A higher IRR means a higher potential rate of growth (return). Therefore, of the two projects, project A with the IRR of 25% is preferable than project B with the IRR of 15%.
Details for Answer C: Project B because a lower IRR represents a shorter payback periodIncorrect. IRR is an internal rate of return. There is no correlation between the IRR and the payback period.
Details for Answer D: Project A because letter "A" comes before "B"Incorrect. This answer choice is obviously nonsense.

Hint: Enterprise environmental factors are conditions, not under the immediate control of the team, that influence, constrain, or direct the project. Correct Answer: C
Explanation: The question describes enterprise environmental factors (EEFs), which are conditions, not under the immediate control of the team, that influence, constrain, or direct the project. These conditions can be internal and/or external to the performing organization. EEFs are one of the inputs to the Develop Project Charter process. The legislation, which imposes overtime pay is an example of a legal requirement which is not under the immediate control of the project team and is external to the organi
Details for Answer A: Project monitoring and reporting methodsIncorrect. Project monitoring and reporting methods are examples of the organizational process assets, while the scenario implies the project sponsor is looking for the enterprise environmental factors.
Details for Answer B: An approved template of a project charterIncorrect. An approved project charter template is an example of an organizational process asset. Whereas, the question refers to enterprise environmental factors.
Details for Answer C: Legislation on overtime pay for the project teamCorrect. Legislation that imposes overtime pay is an example of a legal requirement. Legal and regulatory requirements are enterprise environmental factors, which are not under the immediate control of the project team.
Details for Answer D: Historical information and lessons learned repositoryIncorrect. Historical information and lessons learned repository are examples of the organizational process assets, while according to the scenario, the project sponsor is interested in the enterprise environmental factors.

Question: The total budget for the project is $50 million, and the actual costs so far are $40 million. The project has a CPI of 0.92 and the SPI of 0.80. What is the estimate at completion that considers both the cost and schedule performance?
Hint: Both the SPI and the CPI influence the remaining work, so the EAC formula must be chosen accordingly. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: In this scenario, the project work that is remaining to be completed is impacted by the fact that there is no flexibility in the project schedule. The completion date must be met. One needs to use a formula for the EAC that considers both the SPI and the CPI. This formula is EAC = AC + [(BAC - EV) / (CPI x SPI)]. The EV is not provided and therefore has to be obtained using the formula CPI = EV / AC. The details of the calculation are below: EV = AC x CPI = 40 x 0.92 = 36.8 EAC = AC + [(BAC - EV
Details for Answer A: $54.35 millionIncorrect. The formula used for this answer is EAC = BAC / CPI. This formula is used when the CPI is expected to be the same for the remainder of the project. However, in this scenario, you do not necessarily expect CPI to be the same for the remainder of the project. Since there is no flexibility on the project completion date, an equation that includes the SPI must be used.
Details for Answer B: $57.94 millionCorrect. In this scenario, there is no flexibility on the scheduled completion date. Therefore, the CPI and SPI influence the remaining work. The correct formula is EAC = AC+ [(BAC-EV) / (CPI x SPI)].
Details for Answer C: $53.20 millionIncorrect. The formula used for this answer is EAC = AC + BAC - EV. This formula is used when the present cost variance was only a one-time event, and the future work will be accomplished at the planned rate. In the scenario described, the CPI has been consistently low, and the SPI needs to be included because the project completion date cannot be adjusted.
Details for Answer D: $17.93 millionIncorrect. The formula used for this answer is ETC = EAC - AC and is the formula for the estimate 'to' complete, not the estimate 'at' completion. The ETC is the expected cost to finish all the remaining project work. The EAC is the expected 'total' cost of completing all project work, which is what is asked for in the question.

What data analysis tool or technique has the project team most likely utilized?
Hint: What data analysis technique associated with the Monitor Risks process utilizes the technical performance measurement of transaction times? Correct Answer: C
Explanation: The scenario implies the team is carrying out the Monitor Risks process. Technical performance analysis is an example of the data analysis tools and techniques that can be used as part of this process. Technical performance analysis compares technical accomplishments during project execution to the schedule of technical achievement. It requires the definition of objective, quantifiable measures of technical performance, which can be used to compare actual results against targets. Such technical
Details for Answer A: Monte Carlo simulationIncorrect. A Monte Carlo simulation is an analysis technique where a computer model is iterated many times and outputs are generated to represent the range of possible outcomes for the project. In this case, the average transaction time has been determined to be 37 minutes rather than a range of possible outcomes.
Details for Answer B: Multicriteria decision analysisIncorrect. Multicriteria decision analysis is a technique that utilizes a decision matrix to provide a systematic analytical approach for establishing criteria, such as risk levels, uncertainty, and valuation, to evaluate and rank many ideas. The project team is comparing the actual project results with the planned targets. This activity is different from the multicr
Details for Answer C: Technical performance analysisCorrect. Technical performance analysis compares technical accomplishments during project execution to the schedule of technical achievement. It requires the definition of objective, quantifiable measures of technical performance, which can be used to compare actual results against targets. Such technical performance measures may include weight, transaction times, num
Details for Answer D: Perform Quantitative Risk AnalysisIncorrect. Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis is the process of numerically analyzing the combined effect of identified individual project risks and other sources of uncertainty on overall project objectives. The question is asking for a tool or technique.

Question: You are working with your project team to obtain administrative closure of a project. The project was performed exclusively with internal resources provided by the functional leaders. Which of the following is most likely to be the last action taken in this scenario?
Answer A: Conducting a survey to measure stakeholder satisfaction
Answer C: Transferring the final product to operations
Hint: At least some of the project team must be available to perform the administrative closure of the project. Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Although some of the project team members may be released before the project has been closed, some of the project team must remain to perform the activities associated with the administrative closure of the project. Even if only the project manager remains on the project, the project manager is considered a member of the project team. Note, the PMBOK® Guide does not specify the order in which activities are performed during the Close Project or Phase process. However, it makes sense that the pro
Details for Answer A: Conducting a survey to measure stakeholder satisfactionIncorrect. This answer choice does represent an action associated with the administrative closure of the project. However, a member of the project team must be available to collect and analyze the survey information. Therefore, measuring stakeholder satisfaction must be completed before the project team can be released.
Details for Answer B: Finalizing open claimsIncorrect. Finalizing open claims is one of the steps completed during the administrative closure of the project. Claims may result as part of the procurement process. The question states that the project was completed exclusively with internal resources, which means that vendors were not used for the project. Therefore, it is not logical that any claims would exist in this s
Details for Answer C: Transferring the final product to operationsIncorrect. The transfer of the software to operations is one of the tasks that should be completed for administrative closure of the project. However, at least some of the project team must perform this activity. Therefore, the project team cannot be released until this activity is complete.
Details for Answer D: Releasing the project teamCorrect. Although some of the project team members may be released before the project has been closed, some of the project team must remain to perform the activities associated with the administrative closure of the project.

Question: You are leading a project to build ten new houses. This project is the second such for the company. The previous project came in on budget and schedule. You have been provided the cost per square foot per home. The same team from the previous project will be working at the same salary rate on your project. Based on the information you have, which of the following is the most appropriate cost estimating tool or technique to use in this scenario?
Hint: Which estimating tool uses both historical data and other project parameters? Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Based on the information provided, you are performing Estimate Costs process. You have access to the salary of your project team, historical data from the previous similar project, and information about some of the parameters of your current project. Parametric estimating uses a statistical relationship between relevant historical data and other variables. For example, construction projects can use square footage as a parameter. Therefore, parametric estimating, unlike the other answer choices,
Details for Answer A: Three-point estimatingIncorrect. To define cost range, three-point estimating uses three estimation scenarios: most likely, optimistic, pessimistic. You are provided the cost elements and are using historical information as well. Therefore, three-point estimating is not your best choice.
Details for Answer B: Vendor bid analysisIncorrect. Vendor bid analysis would be used by a buyer to evaluate costs of bids from multiple vendors. The question does not suggest that the project involves multiple vendors.
Details for Answer C: Analogous estimatingIncorrect. Analogous estimating uses historical data such as cost and duration from previous projects to estimate costs of the current project. Analogous estimating is typically used when there is limited information about the current project. In this scenario, the information is available, and therefore, analogous estimating is not the best tool for the project manager to use.
Details for Answer D: Parametric estimatingCorrect. Parametric estimating is the best technique to use when the information available about the current project is coupled with the historical information from the previous project.

Question: project manager is nearing the end of a product development project for a large consumer goods manufacturer. Several vendors produced key deliverables for the project, and there are no open claims. The project manager is now carrying out all of the activities associated with project closure and is following all project management best practices. Which of the following tasks should the project manager perform?
Answer B: Formally sign off the project deliverables
Hint: How are the finalized project documents and materials archived? Correct Answer: D
Explanation: The question indicates that the Close Project or Phase process is underway. During project closure, the organizational process assets are updated to include finalized project documents, operational and support documents, project or phase closure documents, and the lessons learned repository. Therefore, of the available choices, updating the organizational process assets is the best answer to the question asked.
Details for Answer A: Close procurements with the vendors--- Incorrect. Procurements are closed as part of the Control Procurements process, not the Close Project or Phase process which is, as implied by the question, the process being carried out in the scenario.
Details for Answer B: Formally sign off the project deliverablesIncorrect. Deliverables that meet the acceptance criteria are formally signed off and approved by the customer or sponsor as part of the Validate Scope process rather than the Close Project or Phase process. In the Close Project or Phase process, the project manager 'confirms' the delivery and formal acceptance of the deliverables. While the difference is
Details for Answer C: Update the enterprise environmental factorsIncorrect. The question implies the Close Project or Phase process is being carried out in the scenario. Updates to the enterprise environmental factors are not among the outputs of this process.
Details for Answer D: Update the organizational process assetsCorrect. Organizational process assets (OPAs) updates are among the outputs of the Close Project or Phase process described in the scenario. The updates to the OPAs may include finalized project documents, operational and support documents, project or phase closure documents, and the lessons learned repository.

Question: You are managing a project that encounters many changes. A stakeholder approaches you asking about the status of a change request that they have recently submitted. What is your best course of action to address the stakeholder's inquiry?
Answer A: Consult the project change management plan
Answer B: Ask the stakeholder to read the latest project status report
Answer D: Send the stakeholder to talk to the project team
Hint: What project document contains the most up-to-date information about the change requests and their statuses? Correct Answer: C
Explanation: - **A change log is a project document that contains information about changes that occur during the** project. The change log is updated as new information about a change request becomes available. For example, if a change request has been approved (or rejected) by the change control board, the status of this change request should be updated accordingly. It is the project manager's responsibility to make sure that the information in the change log is up-to-date and communicated to the stakehold

Details for Answer B: Ask the stakeholder to read the latest project status reportIncorrect. While the project status report may include information about the status of the change requests, this information may be not up-to-date. From the moment the project status report has been published, the status of any change request can change. For example, a change control board has just approved (or rejected) a change request
Details for Answer C: Consult the project change logCorrect. A change log is a project document that stores information about changes that occur during the project and their statuses. When a project is properly managed, the changes are logged, and their statuses are updated in a timely manner.
Details for Answer D: Send the stakeholder to talk to the project teamIncorrect. Although project team members may know about the status of a change request, sending the stakeholder to the team will likely interrupt the team's work. Also, the project team itself is not necessarily involved in executing all approved change requests. It's the project manager’s responsibility to communicate with the stakeholders and let t

The question indicates that the knowledge gained came from the project documents of the current phase, which suggests that the knowledge was

Details for Answer C: Submit a change request to update the lessons learned registerIncorrect. In this case, the project has been initiated by the company president. Therefore, of the choices provided, obtaining final formal acceptance of the project deliverables is what you should achieve when meeting with the president and operations management. Note, the
Details for Answer A: Understanding of the specific features of the vending machineIncorrect. Question: You have developed a project schedule, but the project completion date falls beyond the deadline defined by the project sponsor. You explained this situation to the sponsor and asked them to provide you with additional funds to bring in more people, but the sponsor refused. What can you do to shorten the project duration?
Hint: Which tool or technique may help in shortening the project duration without applying additional resources? Correct Answer: B
Explanation: Fast tracking is a schedule compression technique that can be used during the Develop Schedule process. In this technique, activities on the critical path that were planned to be performed sequentially are performed in parallel for some or all of their duration, thus shortening the overall project length. While no additional resources are required while applying fast tracking, the project manager should be aware that fast tracking may result in rework and an increased risk.
Details for Answer A: Crash the project--- Incorrect. Crashing the project requires additional resources. The project sponsor refused to provide funds to bring in more people.
Details for Answer B: Fast track the projectCorrect. Fast tracking is a schedule compression technique in which critical path project activities that were planned to be executed sequentially are performed in parallel resulting in a shorter project duration.
Details for Answer C: Apply resource levelingIncorrect. Resource leveling usually increases the project duration.
Details for Answer D: Do nothingIncorrect. Doing nothing knowing that the project will not be completed on time is not a responsible choice of project manager.

An approved change request is required to utilize management reserves and, therefore, cannot be used at the project manager's discretion. Since the management reserves

Yo

By asking for a change in the budget, you increase the chances of the company's successful launch into a new area of retail. As the project manager, you are responsible for the success of the project. Therefore, you need to mo

Question: You have just completed the Collect Requirements process with a group of senior executives and your team. You begin developing the scope statement and realize that you need more clarification on a specific component of the deliverable. What document should you reference to find the person who requested the specific component?
Hint: There are two outputs to the Collect Requirements process. One is the requirements documentation. What is the other output? Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Part of the Collect Requirements process is to link product requirements from their origin (including the person who requested the requirement) to the deliverables that satisfy them. Attributes, including source, associated with each requirement are recorded in the requirements traceability matrix. You may need to contact the person (or source) who requested the requirement to clarify the specifications for the scope statement, as in this scenario, but also in cases where the stakeholder should
Details for Answer A: Stakeholder engagement assessment matrixIncorrect. The stakeholder engagement assessment matrix is typically used in the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process to compare current and desired stakeholder engagement levels required for successful project delivery. It does not indicate who requested a requirement.
Details for Answer B: Responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)Incorrect. RAM is a general term for a grid that shows what project resources are assigned to each work package. RAM illustrates connections between work packages, or activities and project team members. It does not indicate who requested a requirement.
Details for Answer C: RACI chartIncorrect. A RACI chart is a common type of RAM that defines the involvement of stakeholders and team members with responsible, accountable, consult and inform statuses for project activities. It does not indicate who requested a requirement.
Details for Answer D: Requirements traceability matrixCorrect. The requirements traceability matrix links product requirements from their origin to the deliverables that satisfy them. Question: project management plan has been approved, and the project is now in execution. The project manager would like to communicate project status to the stakeholders. Which of the following should the project manager send to stakeholders to achieve her immediate goal?
Hint: Which answer choice represents the physical or electronic representation of work performance information compiled in project documents, intended to generate decisions, actions, or awareness? Correct Answer: A
Explanation: Project reporting is the act of collecting and distributing project information. Work performance reports are the physical or electronic representation of work performance information compiled in project documents, intended to generate decisions, actions, or awareness. Work performance reports would allow the project
Details for Answer A: Work performance reportCorrect. The act of collecting and distributing project information to groups of stakeholders is known as project reporting. To complete the project reporting, the work performance reports, which contain the work performance data that is collected during execution and analyzed and put into context during the Monitor and Control Project Work process, should be sent to the sta
Details for Answer B: Work performance dataIncorrect. Work performance data are the raw observations and measurements collected while project activities are being performed. Work performance data is not in a useful or appropriate form until it is analyzed in the various controlling processes to create work performance reports.
Details for Answer C: Communications management planIncorrect. The communications management plan is a component of the project management plan that describes how project communications will be planned, structured, implemented, and monitored for effectiveness. The plan does not contain information on project performance.
Details for Answer D: Project management planIncorrect. The project management plan is the document that describes how the project will be executed, monitored and controlled, and closed. Question: The project team that you are managing is entirely virtual and distributed all over the country. You know that as the project progresses, your team will learn things and gather more information about the project. You would like to ensure all that knowledge and information does not get lost. By managing project knowledge, you can improve current project outcomes as well as support future projects. Which of the following will you need to remember when managing knowledge on this project?
Answer A: Managing project knowledge only involves documenting information so it can be shared.
Answer B: Managing project knowledge only involves obtaining lessons learned.
Answer C: Explicit knowledge can be easily shared but isn't always understood correctly.
Answer D: Tacit knowledge is easy to organize and index.
Hint: The information contained in lessons learned is an example of explicit knowledge. Correct Answer: C
Explanation: Knowledge is commonly split into two forms. Tacit knowledge is personal and hard to express. It involves the collective know-how, techniques, and expertise gained from one's experiences. Tacit knowledge is difficult to organize and is typically shared through conversations and interactions between people. Explicit knowledge can be expressed in words, numbers, and symbols and therefore can be easily documented and shared. However, explicit knowledge lacks context and is open to different interpre
Details for Answer A: Managing project knowledge only involves documenting information so it can be shared.Incorrect. Managing project knowledge is not limited to only documenting explicit knowledge and information so it can be shared. Rather, managing project knowledge is concerned with managing 'both' explicit and tacit knowledge. While explicit knowledge can be expressed in words, numbers, and symbols allowing it to
Details for Answer B: Managing project knowledge only involves obtaining lessons learned.Incorrect. Managing project knowledge is concerned with managing both tacit and explicit knowledge. Lessons learned, an example of explicit knowledge, can be easily documented. Without context, however, lessons learned can be misunderstood; therefore, there is more involved in managing project knowledge than recording lessons learn
Details for Answer C: Explicit knowledge can be easily shared but isn't always understood correctly.Correct. Explicit knowledge can be expressed in words, numbers, and symbols and therefore can be easily documented. Explicit knowledge, however, lacks context and is open to different interpretations. Consequently, it can be easily misunderstood.
Details for Answer D: Tacit knowledge is easy to organize and index.Incorrect. Tacit knowledge resides in the minds of people and is best shared through conversations and interactions between people. This type of knowledge, such as beliefs and experience, is personal and difficult to express, organize, and index.

Question: You have just been assigned to a new project which will begin in the near future. The project sponsor has informed you that this project will require rigorous change control. When will you develop the change management plan?
Answer A: Along with the processes of the Initiating Process Group
Answer B: As part of the Develop Project Management Plan process
Answer C: As an output of the Plan Change Management process
Answer D: During the Perform Integrated Change Control process
Hint: The change management plan is created as part of project planning. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: The change management plan is a component of the project management plan that establishes the change control board, documents the extent of its authority, and describes how the change control system will be implemented. Most components of the project management plan are produced as the output from other processes, but the change management plan is created as part of the Develop Project Management Plan process.
Details for Answer A: Along with the processes of the Initiating Process GroupIncorrect. The Initiating Process Group includes the Develop Charter process and the Identify Stakeholders process. The change management plan is developed as part of the --- Planning Process Group and not the Initiating Process Group.
Details for Answer B: As part of the Develop Project Management Plan processCorrect. The change management plan is a component of the project management plan and is developed as part of the Develop Project Management Plan process.
Details for Answer C: As an output of the Plan Change Management processIncorrect. 'Plan Change Management' is not a process specified in the PMBOK® Guide.
Details for Answer D: During the Perform Integrated Change Control processIncorrect. The change management plan is a critical input into the Perform Integrated Change Control process and must be completed before this process can be executed. The change management plan is created during project planning rather than during the Perform Integrated Change Control process which occurs later in the project as part of the Moni

Question: project manager has just completed the development of the project management plan and now needs the plan to be reviewed and signed off by the key stakeholders. What should the project manager do to find the names of these individuals?
Hint: What is the best source of information regarding the identification, assessment, and classification of project stakeholders? Correct Answer: B
Explanation: Ideally, the correct answer could have been worded as, “Review the stakeholder register.” However, this option is not provided. Instead, a wider term, ‘project documents’, is used. Project documents are not part of the project management plan; however, along with the project management plan, they are necessary to manage the project effectively. The stakeholder register is among the project documents. It should have been created as part of the Identify Stakeholders process during project initiati
Details for Answer A: Check the lessons learned repositoryIncorrect. The lessons learned repository may or may not have information from past projects regarding the approval of the project management plan. However, this information will not likely have the current roles and responsibilities of key stakeholders.
Details for Answer B: Review project documentsCorrect. A stakeholder register is one of the project documents. The stakeholder register includes the identification, assessment, and classification of project stakeholders. The project manager should review the stakeholder register to determine who are the relevant stakeholders that need to attend the meeting and invited them as necessary.
Details for Answer C: Contact the human resources departmentIncorrect. The human resource department may have contact information for the identified stakeholders. However, the department is unlikely to have the information on 'who' the stakeholders are for this (or any other) project.
Details for Answer D: Ask the project sponsorIncorrect. The project sponsor may be aware of the names of individuals associated with the identified roles. However, this is not a good use of time nor is the project sponsor the best source for this information. The project manager should use the resources which are already available whenever possible.

Question: project manager is provided a fixed budget, but the cost estimates for the equipment required for the project were 30% higher than expected. What would be the best technique to find alternatives that can be presented to the project sponsors for consideration to lower the cost and meet their budget?
Hint: When assessing cost, think of inherent or intrinsic worth. Correct Answer: B
Explanation: As the project manager, you look for ways to optimize the value of the project, especially working with cost constraints. Value engineering (also known as value analysis) is an example of product analysis, a technique used in the Define Scope process, which is described in the question. Value engineering is a way to look in-depth at all project elements (in this case, equipment) to improve cost efficiencies without loss of performance. Two of the answer choices, the plan cost management and the
Details for Answer A: Plan cost managementIncorrect. Plan cost management is not a technique, but a component of the project management plan that describes the approach to the project's cost management.
Details for Answer B: Value engineeringCorrect. Value engineering is a type of product analysis technique that "supports cost reduction activities by relating the cost of individual components to their function contributions."
Details for Answer C: Fishbone diagramIncorrect. A fishbone diagram (also known as a cause-and-effect diagram) is a technique that illustrates the causes of a problem in separate branches which help determine the main cause of the issue. It does not evaluate the value of a project's components nor consider alternatives.
Details for Answer D: Basis of estimatesIncorrect. The basis of estimates is not a technique, but a project document with details on how estimates were derived.

Question: You are finalizing your project. As you start this process, you need to gain a clear understanding of the project's approval requirements and who will sign off on the project. What should you do to find this information?
Hint: What project document is issued by the sponsor and formally authorizes the existence of the project? Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The Close Project or Phase is the process described in the question. The project charter is one of the inputs used in the Close Project or Phase process. The project charter documents the project success criteria, the approval requirements, and who will sign off on the project. The project charter is the document that is most useful in this scenario, and reviewing this document is what you should do.
Details for Answer A: Review the project charterCorrect. The project charter documents the project success criteria, the approval --- requirements, and who will sign off on the project.
Details for Answer B: Check the business caseIncorrect. The business case is a document that outlines the business need and the cost- benefit analysis that justifies the project. The business case does not contain information on approval requirements or who will sign off on the project.
Details for Answer C: Consult the benefits management planIncorrect. The benefits management plan outlines the target benefits that justify the project, but it does not document the approval requirements or who will sign off on the project.
Details for Answer D: Review the responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)Incorrect. The responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) is a grid that shows the project resources assigned to each work package. The RAM does not contain the project success criteria, the approval requirements, or who will sign off on the project.

Question: As you are monitoring the cost baseline for your project, you notice that project expenses are higher than expected at this point in the project. What is the most likely cause of the variance?
Hint: Proper physical resource management is necessary for successful completion of a project. Correct Answer: D
Explanation: Physical resource management is concentrated in allocating and using the physical resources such as materials, equipment, and supplies efficiently and effectively. Organizations should have data on how much inventory to keep on hand as the project progresses. Failing to manage and control resources efficiently can be a source of increased costs in a project. In the scenario described, you have determined expenses are higher than expected. One of the causes for increased costs is failing to maint
Details for Answer A: Overestimated material costsIncorrect. Estimating material costs is completed as part of Project Cost Management to develop a project budget. Overestimating material costs would lead to a higher approved budget and, therefore, a higher cost baseline, i.e., a smaller variance, and would not cause increased expenses.
Details for Answer B: Lack of stakeholder supportIncorrect. While stakeholder support is essential for the success of a project, it is hard to find the direct correlation between the stakeholder support and higher than expected project expenses. Another answer choice better addresses the question asked.
Details for Answer C: An incomplete milestone listIncorrect. A milestone list is a project document that identifies all project milestones. The existence or lack of a milestone list would unlikely cause project expenses to be higher than expected.
Details for Answer D: Excess inventoryCorrect. Keeping too much inventory may result in high operational costs and higher expenses. The increased cost of additional supplies could cause a deviation from the cost baseline.

Question: While reviewing the quality management plan, you notice that the cost of quality specified in the plan includes a wrong component. Which component did you likely notice?
Hint: One of the answer choices is a made up term. Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The quality management plan is the main output of the Plan Quality Management process. As part of this process, the cost of quality associated with the project can be determined to reflect the appropriate balance for investing in the cost of prevention and appraisal to avoid failure costs. Cost of quality is an example of the data analysis technique that is associated with this process. The cost of quality includes prevention costs, appraisal costs, and failure costs. ‘Change costs’ are not spec
Details for Answer A: Change costsCorrect. 'Change costs' are not a recognized component of the cost of quality. Therefore, including an unrecognized element in the cost of quality is most likely the identified problem.
Details for Answer B: Appraisal costsIncorrect. Appraisal costs are costs related to evaluating, measuring, auditing, and testing the products, deliverables, or services of the specific project. Appraisal costs are included in the cost of quality.
Details for Answer C: Failure costsIncorrect. Failure costs are costs related to nonconformance of the products, deliverables, or services to the needs or expectations of the stakeholders. Failure costs are an element of the cost of quality.
Details for Answer D: Prevention costsIncorrect. Prevention costs are costs related to the prevention of poor quality in the products, deliverables, or services of the specific project. Prevention costs should be included in the cost of quality.

Question: You are leading a project team with the development of the stakeholder engagement plan. Using a data representation technique, you have created the diagram below, and you are sharing it with your team. How might you explain the purpose of this diagram?

Answer D: Displays results of stakeholder analysis comparing to other organizations or other projects
Hint: Which answer choice describes the data representation technique of mind mapping? Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The question states that the project team is developing the stakeholder engagement plan, which suggests that the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process is being performed. The diagram presented in the question is an example of a data representation technique referred to as mind mapping, which may be used during the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process. Mind mapping is used to visually organize information about stakeholders and their relationship to each other and the organization.

The diagram presented in the question is an example of a data representation technique referred to as mind mapping. Mind mapping is used to visually organize information about stakeholders and their relationship to each other and the organization.
Details for Answer B: Supports comparison between the current engagement levels of stakeholders and thedesired engagement levels Incorrect. The diagram presented in the question does not provide stakeholder engagement levels, making this answer choice incorrect.
Details for Answer C: Identifies underlying reasons for the level of support of project stakeholders--- Incorrect. Root cause analysis identifies underlying reasons for the level of support of project stakeholders. The diagram presented in the question does not indicate reasons for the level of support of project stakeholders. Therefore, this answer choice can be eliminated.
Details for Answer D: Displays results of stakeholder analysis comparing to other organizations or other projectsthat are considered to be world class Incorrect. This answer choice describes benchmarking which is an example of a data gathering technique that can be used in the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process. Question: project manager conducted a walkthrough with the sponsors to obtain approval for the project deliverables. The sponsors were very pleased with the completed scope and after the inspection sent a letter praising the project team for exceeding their expectations of quality. Which of the following is least likely to be updated as the result of this letter?
Hint: Project documents are updated with the results of the Validate Scope process to assist with the management of the ongoing project as well as future projects. Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The scenario indicates that the project manager has carried out the Validate Scope process. In this process, the project manager reviews verified deliverables from the Control Quality process with the customer or sponsor. The inspection may take the form of a review or walkthrough. Once the deliverables have been signed off as meeting acceptance criteria, several project documents, such as the lessons learned register, the requirements documentation, and the requirements traceability matrix can
Details for Answer A: Quality management planCorrect. The quality management plan documents how applicable policies will be implemented to achieve the quality objectives of the project. There is nothing in the scenario that indicates that any of the policies or their implementation should be modified as a result of the successful inspection.
Details for Answer B: Lessons learned registerIncorrect. The lessons learned register is updated with information on challenges encountered with ideas on how to avoid them in the future. This register is also updated to highlight approaches that worked especially well. The project manager should add the lessons learned about the efforts of managing quality and validating deliverables that led to the sponsors' praise.
Details for Answer C: Requirements traceability matrixIncorrect. The requirements traceability matrix is a grid that connects each requirement to the deliverable that fulfills it. The matrix would be updated with the validation results, making this choice an incorrect answer.
Details for Answer D: Requirements documentationIncorrect. During the inspection, the client or sponsor compares each requirement to the verified deliverables. As an output, the project manager should update the requirements documentation with the validation results, including client signoff, change requests, waivers of requirements and cases where quality exceeds requirements. As a project document that would be updat

Question: You work as a project manager for an organization that has a directive type of project management office (PMO). The PMO requires you to document relevant information in the lessons learned register. What is the main purpose of preparing this documentation?
Answer A: To enable the ongoing improvement of products, services, and processes
Answer B: To capture issues and risks and document how they were handled
Answer C: To ensure quality policies and procedures are implemented on the project
Answer D: To satisfy the requirements of the project management office (PMO)
Hint: The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project. Correct Answer: A
Explanation: Continuous improvement, sometimes called continual improvement, is the ongoing improvement of products, services or processes through incremental and/or breakthrough improvements. The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used to improve performance in the current project and entered into the lessons learned repository for use in future projects. The lessons learned register is produced as an output of the Manage Project

The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used to improve products, services, and processes in the current project and entered into the lessons learned repository for use in future projects.
Details for Answer B: To capture issues and risks and document how they were handledIncorrect. While the lessons learned register might include issues and risks and how they were handled, it is not limited to issues and risks. The lessons learned register is used to document the knowledge gained during the project to improve future performance. Additionally, issues are primarily recorded in the issue log and risks are
Details for Answer C: To ensure quality policies and procedures are implemented on the projectIncorrect. Ensuring quality policies and procedures are implemented on the project is part of the activities associated with the Project Quality Management Knowledge Area. Documenting information in the lessons learned register serves another purpose.
Details for Answer D: To satisfy the requirements of the project management office (PMO)Incorrect. Although complying with organizational requirements might motivate a project manager to maintain a lessons learned register, the question is asking for the main purpose of preparing this documentation. The purpose of preparing and maintaining the lessons learned register is to document the knowledge gained during the proj

Question: To determine the project needs and structure, the project manager has had several meetings with the project sponsor and some of the key stakeholders. As a result, the project manager has completed the development of a document that describes the project purpose, quantifiable criteria that must be met by the project, and key deliverables. What should the project manager do next?
Hint: What is required before a project is formally authorized? Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The document described in the scenario is the project charter. The project charter documents high-level information on the project and describes the quantifiable criteria that must be met by the project as well as the key deliverables. Chartering a project validates the alignment of the project to the strategy and ongoing work of the organization. An 'approved' project charter formally authorizes the project and allows the project manager to apply organizational resources to project activities.
Details for Answer A: Get approval for the documentCorrect. The document described in the scenario is the project charter. The project charter documents high-level information on the project, the quantifiable criteria that must be met by the project, and the key deliverables. To authorize the project officially, the project charter should be approved.
Details for Answer B: Develop the project management planIncorrect. The project management plan is a comprehensive document that defines the project work and how the work will be performed. The project charter is an input to the Develop Project Management Plan process. Since the scenario implies the project charter has not yet been approved, the development of the project management plan cannot begin.
Details for Answer C: Develop the project charterIncorrect. The project charter documents high-level information on the project and describes the quantifiable criteria that must be met by the project. The scenario implies the document the project manager helped develop is the project charter. Since the project charter has already been developed, this answer choice is incorrect.
Details for Answer D: Identify stakeholders and analyze themIncorrect. The document described in the scenario is the project charter. Since the project charter authorizes both the project and the project manager, getting the charter approved should precede any other activity, including the identification of stakeholders and stakeholder analysis.

Question: You as a project manager have outsourced a portion of your work to a vendor with whom neither you nor the sponsor has worked with before. You have a cost-reimbursable contract with the seller. The sponsor, on more than one occasion, has discussed his concerns with you that the seller could easily increase their profits with this type of agreement. What should you tell the sponsor to decrease his apprehensions?
Answer A: You audit every invoice to ensure the costs are applicable and chargeable to your project.
Answer B: You do not expect any changes to the scope.
Answer C: The seller has the risk with this type of contract.
Answer D: With this type of contract, the seller agrees to a set reimbursement amount, so the final
Hint: Part of the Control Procurements process is monitoring payments to the sellers. Correct Answer: A
Explanation: The Control Procurements process has a financial management component that involves monitoring payments to the seller. One of the chief concerns is making sure the charges from the seller represent the work that has been completed. By auditing the seller invoices, you can ensure the seller is charging you correctly for the work that has been completed. In this scenario, the audit should help ease the sponsor's concerns of being overcharged by the seller.
Details for Answer A: You audit every invoice to ensure the costs are applicable and chargeable to your project.Correct. A cost-reimbursable contract involves payments (cost reimbursements) to the seller for all costs incurred for completed work. By auditing every invoice, you can ensure that the costs that have been billed are aligned with the work that has been completed and any extra charges will be investigated. Th
Details for Answer B: You do not expect any changes to the scope.Incorrect. A cost-reimbursable contract involves payments (cost reimbursements) to the seller for all costs incurred for completed work. This type of agreement is used when the scope of the work is expected to change, and costs cannot be estimated accurately enough to use a fixed-price contract, which has less buyer cost risk. If you did not expect change
Details for Answer C: The seller has the risk with this type of contract.Incorrect. A cost-reimbursable contract involves payments (cost reimbursements) to the seller for all costs incurred for completed work. In this type of agreement, you, as the buyer, have the most cost risk because the total costs are unknown.
Details for Answer D: With this type of contract, the seller agrees to a set reimbursement amount, so the finalcosts to your project are pre-determined. Incorrect. According to the document, a new problem that was logged yesterday by a project team member indicates that delays have just exceeded the established schedule variance threshold and corrective action is required. Answer B: Record the disposition of the change request

The question is describing the issue log as opposed to the change log, and the disposition of change requests is not recorded in the issue log.
